notes: 4 evals, 1 run each. With-skill 100% (21/21). Baseline 52% (11/21). The gap is the record-class discipline, nothing else.

Differentiation is strongest on the SNAPSHOT trap (eval-1: baseline 20%) and weakest on the terminal-LOSS (eval-3: baseline 60%). Reason: gameWon:false on a solitaire-win-* is intuitively a loss, so the baseline gets eval-3 mostly right; but it trusts gameWon:false UNIVERSALLY, which is exactly why it mislabels the solitaire-game snapshot as a loss in eval-1/eval-2. The skill's unique value is the win-file-vs-game-file type precision.
Non-discriminating: per-file attribution (model/build/seed) and build-heterogeneity detection pass in both configs. The skill adds little there; a capable baseline reads those fine.

Baseline broke the treat-as-new instruction on evals 0/2 by reading the manifest/store (noticed fixtures already ingested). It still failed the snapshot calls, so the delta is not an artifact of that leak.
Efficiency: with-skill is faster and fewer tokens on evals 0-2 (triage_export.py short-circuits hand exploration); eval-3 is ~even since both must read one synthetic file closely.
